ops.body:
# Broker is the sole key-release path (v0.12.0 rationale)

`feat!: v0.12.0` (commit 1f5e50aa5631) made two structural decisions that later changes must not casually undo:

## 1. One key path through internal/auth.Broker

The `auth.Broker` owns grants, the key cache, the promptGate, per-browser singleflight, consent nonces, and EVERY release path, behind four entry points: `Key`, `LocalKeys`/`LocalKeysWithState` (budgeted), `Status`, `Classify`. Nothing else releases keys. Daemon handlers are thin wrappers over these — do not add a handler that touches the cache or helper directly; route it through the Broker so singleflight, grant TTL capping, and consent routing stay coherent.

## 2. Presence refusal is a cache-internal event, never a public error

The Secure-Enclave presence-refusal fatal (`cache-unwrap exited 3` / OSStatus -25308) is structurally gone: `internal/cache` is rewritten around heap-allocated epochs (ENCLAVE ↔ MEMORY, CLOSED terminal; pointer identity is the generation test, closing the Get/Put ABA windows — backlog b2e6be7). `Get` demotes+evicts+misses on a refused unwrap; `Put` heals at most once per call, re-validates its epoch after every helper subprocess, publishes only under the current epoch, and reports the epoch kind it published under so TTLs derive from reality (MEMORY-published entries and grants are capped at the degraded TTL; caps only ever shorten). Degrade-not-die: code above the cache must never see or branch on presence refusal.

## Consequences for future edits

- Routed consent fails over past unavailable/unreachable approvers to the next live peer; denied stays terminal; protocol errors fail loud. Peer probes and consent legs are bounded. Don't reintroduce unbounded legs.
- Union peer reads return a typed `PeerReadError` rendered as three honest warning cases (peer stderr surfaced verbatim on timeouts) — keep errors typed at this boundary.
- Requires synckit ≥ v0.12.0; the cookiesync rpc wire surface stayed byte-compatible with v0.11.x peers.
- F7 SHIPPED (cookiesync v0.13.1, main 114fefe8, 2026-07-14) — the two follow-ups this doc used to park are closed: approver-side presence-probe failures classify retryable-unavailable (`approverUnavailableError` wraps the ModeApprover probe error in `Broker.Key`, so `handleRequestConsent` answers `{"status":"unavailable"}` and the requestor's `routedRelease` fails over), and whoami-leg probe flakes route around via `probeRoutesAround` (DeadlineExceeded or any `*hostregistry.SSHError`) in BOTH the `routedRelease` candidate loop and `routesConsent`'s hard-route preflight (a probe-shaped error means peer-not-live; the existing presence rule then applies). Whoami parse failures and consent-leg hard exits stay loud; `unionReadTimeout` stays 15s by design. A total union shutout returns `noContributionError` — the accumulated warnings plus a "consent may be pending on <hosts>" summary for timed-out peers — instead of the misleading "run cookiesync auth". Pinned by `TestHandleRequestConsentProbeErrorReturnsUnavailable`, `TestRoutedReleaseFailsOverWhoamiErrorApprover`, `TestKeyHardRouteWhoamiSSHErrorMatchesNotLivePeer`, `TestUnionTotalShutoutErrorCarriesWarnings`.
